claims.fieldOrBehavior: git status --porcelain=v1 | wc -l | grep -qx 26 # 20 modified + 6 untracked, byte-identical file set to iteration 1/2; only evals/integration/test_hook_category_behaviors.sh edited since iteration-2 record-evidence per command-log.jsonl timeline

true # mutation check: Pass-2 substring-scan loop in remedyForCommand disabled on a scratch copy of config-protection.js -> interpreter-write-to-state.json assertion falls back to shell-profile remedy and FAILS; restored copy -> PASS with advance-state remedy; production file untouched (sha256 e60b712c unchanged) - see verification report iteration 3
sha256sum scripts/hooks/config-protection.js | grep -q e60b712c09eb4023c16f3c87755f12b8625fe65cf1e481dfd6cf8389f590e8 # production hook byte-identical after mutation probe
npm run test:unit # 15/15
npm run eval:static # exit 0 all suites
bash evals/integration/test_hook_category_behaviors.sh # 26 checks incl Pass-2 mutation-pinned assertion
bash evals/integration/test_runtime_adapter_activation.sh # 17-skills AC5 + AC2 dependency-enforcement canary
bash evals/static/test_universal_bundles.sh # 43/43 incl AC6 collision detector
bash evals/integration/test_flow_kit_repository.sh && bash evals/integration/test_local_flow_kit_install.sh
bash evals/integration/test_runtime_adapter_activation.sh
grep -q "\"kit_id\": \"knowledge\"" kits/builder/kit.json && node build/src/cli/kit.js inspect kits/builder --json >/dev/null
test -f docs/adr/0019-kit-dependency-ownership.md && grep -q 0019 docs/adr/0007-flow-skill-kit-tool-boundary.md
bash evals/static/test_universal_bundles.sh
bash evals/integration/test_hook_category_behaviors.sh
! grep -rn "owns root state.json" kits/ agents/ --include=*.md --include=*.json
bash evals/integration/test_validate_artifacts_portability.sh

AC1 `kit-dependency-shape-and-install`: `kit.json` supports a `dependencies: [{kit_id, reason}]` array; malformed entries (bad kebab-case id, self-reference, duplicate) are rejected as hard errors at `flow-agents kit install`/`inspect` time via `validateKitRepository`; `flow-agents kit install` prints an actionable (non-blocking) warning when a declared dependency is not present in the destination's local registry

AC2 `kit-dependency-activation-enforcement`: `flow-agents kit activate` fails (non-zero exit, `errors[]` populated) when a declared dependency's `kit_id` is not present among the union of built-in catalog kits and locally-installed kits at activation time

AC3 `builder-knowledge-dependency-declared`: `kits/builder/kit.json` declares `dependencies: [{"kit_id":"knowledge","reason":"learning-review invokes knowledge-capture for durable knowledge storage"}]`; `flow-agents kit inspect kits/builder` no longer lists `dependencies` in `third_party_extensions` or `targets` (recognized as a known metadata field, not an unknown extension namespace)

AC4 `dependency-ownership-adr`: a new ADR (`docs/adr/0019-*.md`) records the Flow-vs-extension-layer ownership decision for kit `dependencies`, citing ADR 0007 (deferred npm-dependency model), ADR 0008 (the "does it interpret the extension?" dividing test), and `flow-kit-container.schema.json`'s `additionalProperties: true` / "ignores-but-permits" design as the basis for not requiring a `@kontourai/flow` change

AC5 `activation-skill-collision-fixed`: `activateCodexLocal` and `activateStrandsLocal` write each kit's skills to `projections/<adapter>/skills/<kit-id>/<skill-dir-name>/SKILL.md` (namespaced by both kit id and skill directory name) instead of colliding at `.../<kit-id>/SKILL.md`

AC6 `bundle-skill-collision-detected`: `npm run build:bundles` fails with a clear, named collision diagnostic (source kit ids + skill name) when two kits (or a top-level `skills/` entry and a kit) declare the same skill directory name, instead of silently keeping only the first

AC7 `sidecar-hook-message-and-scope-fixed`: `config-protection.js` (a) never tells an agent to "disable the config-protection hook" for any sidecar-owned path (`current.json`, `<slug>/state.json`, `<slug>/trust.bundle`, `delivery/trust.bundle`, `delivery/trust.checkpoint.json`) — instead names the sanctioned `npm run workflow:sidecar -- <command>` remedy; (b) no longer blocks read-only tool calls (`Read`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `LS`, `NotebookRead`) against these paths

AC8 `sidecar-governance-wording-fixed`: `kits/builder/skills/deliver/SKILL.md`, `execute-plan/SKILL.md`, `plan-work/SKILL.md`, `agents/tool-planner.json`, and `agents/tool-verifier.json` all state that `state.json`/`handoff.json` updates — including the orchestrator's own — are routed through `npm run workflow:sidecar -- advance-state`/`init-plan`, never through direct Write/Edit tool calls

AC9 `sidecar-portability-fixed`: `flow-agents-validate-artifacts` resolves its JSON Schemas relative to its own installed package location, not `process.cwd()`, and runs successfully via `npx --package=@kontourai/flow-agents -- flow-agents-validate-artifacts --require-sidecars <dir>` from a directory with no local `schemas/` folder
